title: Apply memory lifecycle upgrade

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Apply the safe memory lifecycle upgrade requested in chat on 2026-05-18.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to AW-managed memory/workspace payload surfaces and planning evidence for this upgrade.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Run the lifecycle apply command, inspect generated changes, validate with doctor and focused inventory/planning checks, and route dogfooding residue.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The upgrade wants to touch product source outside .agentic-workspace managed surfaces, requires review-blocked changes, or validation cannot prove the lifecycle state is healthy.
canonical_core.next_action: Run the memory lifecycle upgrade apply command, then verify with doctor and changed-path checks.
proof_expectations: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules memory --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Memory doctor no longer reports a safe memory lifecycle repair for this target.
Any dogfooding friction discovered during the upgrade is routed.
canonical_core.continuation_owner: #1051 for wording/product friction; otherwise none for this requested apply task

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. closure_check.why this decision is honest: The requested action was only to apply the upgrade, and the lifecycle and planning checks are clean afterward.

closure_check.reopen trigger: A later doctor run reports new lifecycle repair/manual review actions.
